title: Pakistani Medical Students Stage Protest at Torkham, Stranded from Afghan Universities
detail: Approximately 30-35 Pakistani medical students enrolled at Aryana Medical University in Jalalabad, Afghanistan (and others studying in Kabul) remained stranded on the Pakistani side of Torkham, unable to cross for their university examinations. Exams were scheduled to begin April 4 at the Jalalabad university. The students staged a protest at the border checkpoint, citing financial hardship from extended waiting costs and academic damage from missing examinations. Pakistani officials had not responded to their appeals. The closure had been in place since the March 27 Afghan sniper incident. The students' predicament highlighted the wider impact of the border closure on ordinary civilians and educational institutions that had developed academic ties across the Durand Line.

title: Pakistan Fires 185 Long-Range Artillery Shells Into Single Kunar District, 178 Additional Strikes Elsewhere
detail: Pakistani forces fired 185 long-range artillery shells into a single district in Kunar province, wounding 10 people. An additional 178 long-range artillery and mortar shells struck other areas of Kunar, bringing the multi-day total to over 500 projectiles fired into the province since April 2. The heavy sustained barrage continued even as Pakistan's delegation participated in China-mediated talks in Urumqi. Afghan officials noted the simultaneous diplomacy and bombardment as evidence of Pakistani bad faith at the negotiating table. The escalating artillery campaign represented the most intensive sustained bombardment of a specific province since the conflict began on February 26.
